text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise-old owl named Oliver. Every evening, he'd perched on a gnarled branch overlooking the quiet town. On this particular night, Oliver pondered the mysteries of existence.

"Who are they? All those fluttering shapes?" Oliver whispered to himself.

One after another, tiny figures skimmed across the moonlit sky. They looked like butterflies, but something was wrong. Their wings weren't quite right—you could tell they hadn't been made perfectly.

Curiosity piqued, Oliver flew down. These weren't normal creatures—they were hybrids! They looked like butterflies, but their antennae were hooked, and they had tiny specks of something glowing in their wings.

One especially luminous butterfly landed on a flower. Oliver sat in silent astonishment. It wasn't just light—it filled him with a warmth he had never felt before.

"Could they... be guides?" Oliver pondered.

He decided he would ask Lady Moon, the old
